sections.title: What is a Complement?
points: The complement of set A is everything that is not in A.
Example: If A = {1, 2}, and the universal set is {1, 2, 3, 4}, then A' = {3, 4}.

sections.title: Visual Test for 'Not'
points: If the question says 'Not Pizza', we exclude the Pizza circle completely.
We include only Burger only and outside.
This is how complement problems are solved visually.

sections.title: Finding Neither
points: When the total is given, add all regions and subtract from the total.
The outside region is the neither group.

sections.title: Not Both (Complement of Intersection)
points: n(S ∩ W)' means everything except the middle region.
This includes left only, right only, and outside.
Example: If 'not both' = 32, then you count all non-middle regions.

cards.fact: All members not inside the given set.

cards.mnemonic: Neither means none of the choices.

cards.fact: Everything except the intersection of two sets.
cards.mnemonic: Not both removes the middle part.

cards.fact: The big box containing all items under discussion.
cards.mnemonic: The universal set is the scene for the whole problem.
